reasoning: The recent value of 0.873 is exceptionally low compared to the 1-year average of ~2.0, placing it well below the linear trendline. Assuming the series exhibits some mean-reverting behavior (as real interest rates typically do), it is highly likely to increase in the near-term. However, there is a prominent downward linear trend (-0.08 per month) due to recent Fed easing cycles lowering real rates. In the short term (May), mean reversion strongly favors an increase (est. 68%). As the forecast horizon extends to July and October, the negative overarching trend cancels out more of the mean reversion effect, pulling the forecast closer to 50% given the increased baseline uncertainties over longer periods. Thus, probabilities regress toward 0.5 for the later dates.
